data.title: Cellyan Biotechnology receives Nasdaq bid price deficiency notice
data.description:
• Cellyan Biotechnology (HKPD) received a Nasdaq notification on January 12, 2026, for non-compliance with the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The deficiency arose after the company’s shares traded below $1 for 30 consecutive business days from November 26, 2025 to January 9, 2026.
  • Cellyan has been granted a 180-day compliance period, until July 13, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The company may pursue measures such as a reverse share split if needed and could qualify for an additional 180-day grace period.

data.title: Shell, Mitsubishi explore potential sales of stakes in LNG Canada - Reuters
data.description:
Shell (SHEL) and Mitsubishi (MSBHF) are exploring sale options for their respective stakes in the C$40B LNG Canada project, Reuters reported Friday.

Shell (SHEL), LNG Canada's largest owner with a 40% stake, reportedly has been working with investment bankers at Rothschild to sound out interested parties in recent weeks, and the company could offload as much as three-quarters of its holding, or 30% of the project.

However, Shell (SHEL) is said to have expressed willingness to consider different options related to its exposure to the project's Phase 1, which is operational, and the proposed Phase 2, given the different risks.

Any buyer for Shell's (SHEL) stake could be committing ~$15B, including the equity stake, debt and capital requirements for Phase 2, according to the report.

Mitsubishi (MSBHF), which holds a 15% stake in LNG Canada, reportedly has hired RBC Capital as it weighs its options, although it is not clear much of its stake the company could market.

LNG Canada started production in June, but has since run into operational problems, and its Train 2 processing unit was down in December.

The project enjoys a supply cost advantage because prices for Canadian natural gas consistently trade at a discount to the U.S. Henry Hub benchmark, but the industry is concerned about a potential global oversupply as new LNG production comes online; Energy Transfer, for example, said last month that it was suspending development of its Lake Charles LNG export facility in Louisiana.

data.title: Lockheed Martin widens gains after ending seventh straight session in green
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT) extended its rally for the seventh straight session on Friday. It closed 0.79% higher at $582.43.

The stock has been rallying since January 8, when it ended 4.34% higher, following U.S. President Donald Trump’s  push for sharply higher military spending. In a Truth Social post, Trump proposed a historic $1.5T defense budget for fiscal year 2027.

On January 9, LMT rose 4.2% to a 52-week high of $543.08 after Truist Securities upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold with a $605 price target, saying Lockheed's FY 2025 underperformance, coupled with attractive valuation, creates a compelling risk/reward profile in 2026.

As per Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, LMT has a Hold rating with a score of 3.23 out of 5. The company has been rated A+ for profitability, but it scored a D+ for valuation and growth. Seeking Alpha, as well as Wall Street analysts, echoed a similar sentiment and issued a Hold call for the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dhierin Bechai said in a recent article that LMT's appeal is fading, with its upside now limited to 9%. Despite his concerns, he had a Buy rating for the stock.

Bechai said LMT’s growth outlook increasingly depends on securing major new defense platforms, such as the Golden Dome project, as free cash flow growth has stalled. He added that share repurchases no longer provide meaningful upside, with essentially all free cash flow already returned to shareholders, while the dividend yield remains unremarkable.

“In terms of upside, we are seeing that Lockheed Martin has roughly the same upside as Northrop Grumman. I have been bullish on the aerospace and defense industry, but we have detected more value in the supply chain, European defense peers, other emerging defense players, and mixed end-market contractors than with the US prime defense contractors,” the analyst said.

data.title: British American Tobacco records gains for seventh straight session
data.description:
British American Tobacco (BTI) extended its winning streak to the seventh straight session after its shares ended 0.24% higher at $58.22 on Friday.

The stock has been ending in the green since January 8. Since then, BTI has gained nearly 8%. In the past year, BTI had added more than 63% compared to the S&P 500’s 16.72% rise.

Last month, British American Tobacco was named as Jefferies' top pick in the global tobacco sector, backed by its view that the company’s shift toward a smoke-free, products-focused portfolio is sustainable and not fully priced in by the market.

As per Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings, it is recommended to Hold the stock. It has a score of 3.11 out of 5, with an A+ grade for profitability but a D- for growth. However, Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts have a Buy call for the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Sensor Unlimited reiterated its Buy call on the stock after the company reaffirmed its 2026 guidance, targeting 3% - 5% revenue and 5% - 8% adjusted EPS growth. The analyst added that BTI’s £1.3bn buyback for FY26 signals improving cash flow and capital flexibility.

“I believe the current valuation understates its EPS growth potential. Several other specifics further skew the return/risk profile, such as the cash yield, buyback guidance, and deleverage targets. These factors point to a total shareholder yield of more than 10%, a downside cushion too wide to ignore,” Sensor Unlimited said.

However, it also noted that BTI, much like the broader tobacco sector, continues to face a structural decline in demand for smokeable cigarettes. Moreover, the FY2025 update indicates that contributions from its new product categories remain modest, despite encouraging growth trends. At the same time, these newer products are being pressured by competition from illicit alternatives.

data.title: Turbo Energy receives Nasdaq notice
data.description:
• Turbo Energy (TURB) said on Friday it has received a Nasdaq notice indicating non-compliance with capital market listing requirements on January 12, 2026.
  • The company fell below the capital market's $2.5M minimum stockholders’ equity requirement, reporting about $1.5M as of June 30, 2025.
  • The company said it is reviewing measures to restore compliance and will file a plan with Nasdaq as required.

data.title: Altria Group continues seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Altria Group (MO) shares continued gains for seven straight sessions, as the stock closed 0.42% higher at $61.84 on Friday.

The Marlboro maker’s shares gained over 10% in the preceding six sessions. On a year-to-date basis, the shares have gained 6%, outpacing the broader market, which rose by 1.45%.

Last month, the FDA issued Marketing Granted Orders for Altria’s six on! Plus moist nicotine pouches, including mint, tobacco, and wintergreen flavors in 6 mg and 9 mg strengths, as part of a pilot program. 

Turning to the Wall Street community, five out of 15 analysts rated MO a Buy and above, eight analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and two recommended Sell or below.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, MO has a Hold rating with a score of 3.17 out of 5. The company has been graded A+ for profitability but is dragged down by a D grade for growth.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts have issued a Buy call for Altria with six analysts rating it Buy and above, while four analysts suggested to Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Ben Holden-Crowther said that Altria remains heavily reliant on traditional cigarettes, with smoke-free products contributing minimally to revenues and earnings. Additionally, oral pouches (on!) also face significant competition from ZYNs (produced by Philip Morris).

“Despite these challenges, MO's ~10x forward multiple appears cheap, and the 8.6x operating income multiple for cigarettes (based on FY24) as well as the firm's well-covered 7.5% dividend yield will be attractive to income-focused investors,”  Holden-Crowther added, rating it a Buy.

Over the past year, the stock rose nearly 20% so far this year, compared to a rise of more than 16% in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Tech Voices: OpenAI to run ads, Micron CEO on AI growth
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks. 

  • OpenAI (OPENAI) said it plans to test placing ads within certain ChatGPT replies, but that AI responses won't be influenced by them.

In a statement, OpenAI said the ads will appear for U.S. adult users of its free and low-cost Go services, with Plus, Pro, and Enterprise subscriptions remaining ad-free. Ads will also not appear in responses on sensitive topics such as politics, health, and mental health.

"To start, we plan to test ads at the bottom of answers in ChatGPT when there’s a relevant sponsored product or service based on your current conversation. Ads will be clearly labeled and separated from the organic answer. You’ll be able to learn more about why you’re seeing that ad, or dismiss any ad and tell us why," OpenAI said in its statement.

OpenAI, which is still private, counts Microsoft (MSFT) as a major backer. 

  • Micron (MU) CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said that AI is driving increased demand for its memory products.

"AI-driven demand is accelerating,” Mehrotra told CNBC during an interview on Friday. “It is real. It is here, and we need more and more memory to address that demand.”

Mehrotra said the company saw growth in the high-teens percentage range for its server memory products by the end of 2025, along with stronger-than-expected growth for PC memory and storage. As a result, the company is working to produce more chips at its existing manufacturing facilities while it builds additional capacity. 

“We see that tightness continuing into 2027, so we see durable industry fundamentals over the foreseeable future, driven by AI demand,” Mehrotra added.

data.title: U.S. stocks post marginal losses, notch weekly decline
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday ended a listless day with marginal losses, with the session echoing the overall movement seen this week.   

The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) fell -0.17% to close at 49,359.33 points. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) slipped -0.06% to conclude at 6,940.01 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) also declined -0.06% to finish at 23,515.39 points.

For the week, the Dow was -0.29%, the S&P was -0.38%, and the Nasdaq was -0.66%.   

In a week in which the economic calendar didn't prove to be much of a catalyst, the focus was on the artificial intelligence trade, which got a shot in the arm from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (TSM) strong results. Chipmakers such as Micron (MU) +7.76%, Lam Research (LRCX) +2.53%, and AMD (AMD) +1.68% were among the top percentage gainers on the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) on Friday.

“Something of a nothingburger this week for the main equity indices – the Dow, Nasdaq, and S&P 500 all closed down a touch on last week, having trended in a sideways rangebound fashion all week," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

"Only the Russell 2000 (RTY) continued to make new highs. We believe this sideways consolidation to be temporary digestion and expect all four indices to run up towards the end of Q1. We are also watching the software/semiconductor play. Software isn’t all as bad as it looks, and semiconductor isn’t all as good as it looks; capital rotation opportunities are shortly to surface, we think,” King added.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields rose across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed to 3.60%, up 2.8 basis points. The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose to 4.23%, adding 5.1 basis points. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased to 4.84%, up 3.7 basis points.

data.title: BOK Financial GAAP EPS of $2.89, revenue of $560.14M
data.description:
• BOK Financial press release (BOKF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.89.
  • Revenue of $560.14M.

data.title: Fed's Jefferson says he sees current rate setting as neutral
data.description:
Federal Reserve Vice Chair Phillip Jefferson said Friday the central bank's monetary policy stance is "well positioned to determine the extent and ‌timing of additional adjustments to our policy rate based on the incoming data, ​the evolving outlook, and the balance of risks."

With the Fed's January meeting coming up in less than two weeks -- deciding whether to cut rates for a fourth straight time -- Jefferson contended the policy rate is already consistent with the neutral rate, the rate that neither stimulates nor hinders economic activity. 

He declined to "prejudge the decision" in remarks prepared for delivery at an event in Florida, though it appears he's signaling a rate pause.

The three quarter-point reductions last year were "the right step ‌to balance ‌the upside risk of persistent above-target inflation and the downside risk of a deteriorating labor market," Jefferson said. "This policy stance puts the economy in a ⁠good position moving forward."

He said he's "cautiously optimistic" about the U.S. economic outlook this year, calling for around 2% growth in the near term, a steady jobless rate and inflation heading sustainably toward the Fed's 2% objective.
